title: Archetype
description: Schema for Archetype Ascriptions — type definition (meta-model). The Archetype's identity is carried by schema.title (DM-enforced identity-bound rule: MUST be non-empty, globally unique among in-effect Archetypes, and MUST NOT change across Ascriptions of the same Definition). schema.title is used as the root identifier in Norm CEL predicates. The schema property carries the governed JSON Schema document. DM persists the schema as-is and assigns $id using the convention gsm://archetypes/{schema.title}/v{version}. DM input = DM output (1:1). Sealed — tenant-defined archetypes MUST NOT extend Archetype.

schema.description: The governed JSON Schema document (draft 2020-12+) defining the structure and semantics of instances typed by this Archetype. MUST root, via allOf, at exactly one GSM base archetype schema. DM validates the schema and persists it as-is. DM assigns $id inside the schema document using the convention gsm://archetypes/{schema.title}/v{version} — deterministic from schema.title (identity) and Ascription version. The schema content may evolve across Ascription versions (new properties, updated constraints). However, the set of properties annotated $gsm:identityBound within this schema IS immutable per Archetype Definition — changing the identity-bound set requires a new Definition. DM-enforced identity rule: schema.title MUST be non-empty, globally unique among in-effect Archetypes, and MUST NOT change across Ascriptions of the same Definition. schema.title is the Archetype's human-readable identity — used as the root identifier in Norm CEL guard/predicate expressions (e.g., 'SecurityProperties.encryptionLevel').
